text: <sup>[1]</sup> Paul, a prisoner for Christ Jesus, and Timothy our brother, to Philemon, our beloved and our co-worker, <sup>[AA]</sup>
to Apphia our sister, <sup>[2]</sup> to Archippus our fellow soldier, and to the church at your house. <sup>[AB]</sup>
Grace to you and peace <sup>[3]</sup> from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ. <sup>[AC]</sup>
<sup>[4]</sup> <sup>[AD]</sup> I give thanks to my God always, remembering you in my prayers,
as I hear of the love and the faith you have in the Lord Jesus and for all the holy ones, <sup>[5]</sup>
so that your partnership in the faith may become effective in recognizing every good there is in us <sup>[6]</sup> that leads to Christ. <sup>[AE]</sup>
For I have experienced much joy and encouragement <sup>[7]</sup> from your love, because the hearts of the holy ones have been refreshed by you, brother. <sup>[AF]</sup>
Therefore, although I have the full right <sup>[8]</sup> in Christ to order you to do what is proper,
I rather urge you out of love, being as I am, Paul, an old man, <sup>[9]</sup> and now also a prisoner for Christ Jesus. <sup>[AG]</sup>
I urge you on behalf of my child Onesimus, whose father I have become in my imprisonment, <sup>[AH]</sup>
who was once useless to you but is now useful <sup>[10]</sup> to [both] you and me.
I am sending him, that is, my own heart, back to you.
I should have liked to retain him for myself, so that he might serve <sup>[11]</sup> me on your behalf in my imprisonment for the gospel, <sup>[AI]</sup>
but I did not want to do anything without your consent, so that the good you do might not be forced but voluntary. <sup>[AJ]</sup>
Perhaps this is why he was away from <sup>[12]</sup> you for a while, that you might have him back forever,
no longer as a slave but more than a slave, a brother, beloved especially to me, but even more so to you, as a man <sup>[13]</sup> and in the Lord. <sup>[AK]</sup>
So if you regard me as a partner, welcome him as you would me.
<sup>[14]</sup> And if he has done you any injustice or owes you anything, charge it to me.
I, Paul, write this in my own hand: I will pay. May I not tell you that you owe me your very self. <sup>[AL]</sup>
Yes, brother, may I profit from you in the Lord. Refresh my heart in Christ.
With trust in your compliance I write to you, knowing that you will do even more than I say.
At the same time prepare a guest room for me, for I hope to be granted to you through your prayers. <sup>[AM]</sup>
Epaphras, <sup>[15]</sup> my fellow prisoner in Christ Jesus, greets you, <sup>[AN]</sup>
as well as Mark, Aristarchus, Demas, and Luke, my co-workers.
The grace of the Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. <sup>[AO]</sup>
